creator: marko.cupac@mimar.rs
text:
(In reply to Ashish SHUKLA from comment #1)

Hi,

I tried to modify your port from 24.6 to 24.7 but it wouldn't build (same errors). I'm building your 24.6 now, will know more tomorrow and let you know.

In the meantime, I tried to compile 24.7 directly, without port, following instruction on:

https://docs.ejabberd.im/admin/install/source/

After installing prerequisites (perhaps something is missing and something is not really needed):

$ pkg prime-origins
devel/automake
devel/cmake-core
lang/erlang
textproc/expat2
devel/gmake
databases/libiodbc
textproc/libyaml
ports-mgmt/pkg
databases/sqlite3
security/sudo
databases/unixODBC

And setting some environment variables:

$ env
(...)
LDFLAGS=-L/usr/local/lib
CPPFLAGS=-I/usr/local/include
CFLAGS=-I/usr/local/include

It appears that I managed to autogen.sh, configure and gmake 24.7:

(inside 24.7 source dir):
./autogen.sh
./configure --enable-user=ejabberd --enable-mysql --enable-pam --enable-redis --enable-pgsql --enable-sqlite --enable-sip --enable-lua --enable-odbc
./gmake

I then tried to run 'gmake relive', and it tried to start but complained:

2024-10-22 22:11:00.206381+02:00 [error] Failed to open socket at [::]:5222 for ejabberd_c2s: protocol not supported 

This is inside IPv4-only jail. After I changed '::' to actual IPv4 address where applicable in _build/relive/conf/ejaberd.yml, it seems that ejabberd started fine with 'gmake relive':

2024-10-22 22:32:04.618900+02:00 [info] ejabberd 24.07 is started in the node ejabberd@localhost in 1.12s

I'd conclude 24.07 should run on FreeBSD but I have no idea how to make port work.

Hope this info helps somehow in producing functional port.
